correctness.justification: All 17/17 acceptance tests pass. The implementation correctly handles recursive merging, override precedence, non-dict type replacement, and produces independent copies. All specified requirements are fully met.

edge_cases.justification: All edge cases are handled: empty dicts on either side, both empty, independence of result from inputs, None values, type mismatches (dict replaced by scalar and vice versa), lists replaced not merged, and deeply nested structures. All 17 acceptance tests pass.

code_quality.justification: Excellent code quality: full type hints (including `dict[str, Any]`), clean and idiomatic Python, meaningful variable names, proper use of `copy.deepcopy` for immutability, concise logic with no unnecessary complexity. The recursive approach is clean and well-structured.

documentation.justification: Comprehensive NumPy-style docstring with Parameters, Returns, Raises, and Examples sections. The docstring clearly explains behavior (override precedence, recursion into sub-dicts, no mutation of inputs). Two concrete examples are provided. Module-level docstring is also present.

test_coverage.justification: Exceptional test coverage: happy path (basic merge, override, recursive, 3-level deep, lists, None, mixed types), edge cases (both empty, empty base, empty override), independence/immutability tests (4 separate tests), error paths (3 ValueError tests), and boundary conditions. 23 tests total covering virtually every scenario.

robustness.justification: Input validation raises clear `ValueError` with descriptive messages for non-dict inputs. Uses `copy.deepcopy` throughout to ensure full independence of the result. Handles all type combinations gracefully. No silent failures or unexpected behavior.
